sources.label: StockTitan IMOS news feed (latest items May 27 / May 20 / May 8, 2026; nothing June 5-9)
Insider Monkey: ChipMOS dividend (US$0.78/ADS) and recent share-price surge

verdicts.rankRationale: No CPO-relevant news since June 6 and the thesis already flags low CPO-specific exposure; a +2.5% drift day on top of +89% YTD without fresh fundamentals shaves residual alpha slightly, but at rank 116 the low placement already reflects that.
verdicts.bull: AI-driven memory demand (April revenue +32.2% YoY, Q1 net profit +187%) keeps backend test/packaging utilization tight, and any spillover into advanced-packaging capacity scarcity benefits even second-tier OSATs.
verdicts.neutral: Solid memory-cycle execution and a raised dividend, but nothing connects ChipMOS specifically to the CPO supply chain; the stock is a generic OSAT cycle play on this list.
verdicts.bear: After ~90% YTD on a $2.2B cap, the memory/display-driver cycle is largely priced; a DDIC or commodity-memory downturn unwinds the rerating with no CPO optionality to cushion it.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May 2026 monthly revenue release expected around June 10; Q2 results in August.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Memory/display-driver cycle rollover after a near-doubling YTD with no CPO-specific revenue stream.

verdicts.rankRationale: Backend packaging/test thesis is intact and supported by the last datapoints (April revenue +32.2% YoY on AI demand), but there is zero new information since the June 8 hold verdict; today's +2.46% is flow, not evidence.
verdicts.bull: Monthly revenue momentum (32.2% YoY in April, 25% YoY in Q1) plus a 27.6% dividend hike signal management confidence that AI-memory test/packaging demand persists into 2H26.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis tracking as expected; the watchlist fit (backend packaging and test) is confirmed by results already in the price, so rank 23 remains appropriate pending the May revenue print.
verdicts.bear: An 89% YTD run on a small-cap OSAT leaves little margin for a soft May revenue print or any deceleration in memory test bookings; liquidity is thin enough that crowding exits move the stock fast.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May 2026 monthly revenue 6-K expected around June 10 confirming or breaking the AI-memory demand cadence.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Monthly revenue deceleration or memory-cycle softness puncturing the post-rerating multiple.
